attachment.content:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ship: Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

233 pass, 0 fail. Slowest: `reset-password` rswag integration (0.28s), `RefreshToken` model specs (0.17s avg).

Anything specific to do, or just sharing results?

Rswag specs in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` are the doc-generating ones. Running tests alone — no docs generated.

Docs generate via:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
```

Output: OpenAPI spec (usually `swagger/v1/swagger.yaml`). Served at `http://localhost:3000/api-docs`.

content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	**ThatCookiesMine** — a healthcare/medical appointment platform built as a monorepo of independent microservices. Each service has its own `.git` directory and is versioned independently.
8	
9	## Services & Ports
10	
11	| Service | Stack | Port | Status |
12	|---------|-------|------|--------|
13	| `auth-api/` | Ruby 3.3.6 + Rails 8.0 (API-only) | 3000 | Active |
14	| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
15	| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
16	| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
17	| `frontend/` | React 19 + Node 22 LTS | 5173 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
18	| `mobile/` | TBD | — | Scaffolded |
19	
20	Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.
21	
22	## Infrastructure / Docker
23	
24	```bash
25	cd infrastructure
26	./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
27	./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
28	./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
29	docker-compose logs -f [service]
30	```
31	
32	Dev access points when running:
33	- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
34	- Admin: http://localhost:3001
35	- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
36	- MailHog: http://localhost:8025
37	
38	## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)
39	
40	```bash
41	cd auth-api
42	bundle install
43	cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
44	bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
45	bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
46	COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
47	bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
48	bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
49	bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
50	bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
51	bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
52	```
53	
54	### Auth API Architecture
55	
56	- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
57	- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
58	- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
59	- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
60	- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
61	- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
62	- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
63	- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`
64	
65	## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)
66	
67	```bash
68	cd admin
69	npm install
70	npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
71	npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
72	npm run lint      # ESLint
73	npm run preview   # Preview production build
74	```
75	
76	### Admin Architecture
77	
78	- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
79	- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
80	- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
81	- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
82	- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
83	- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod
84	
85	## Notification Service (Go)
86	
87	Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).
88	
89	```bash
90	cd notification-service
91	go build ./...
92	go vet ./...
93	# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
94	cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
95	```
96	
97	### Notification Service Architecture
98	
99	- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
100	- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
101	- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
102	- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP
103	
104	## Dev Setup
105	
106	Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:
107	
108	```bash
109	cd dev-setup
110	./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
111	./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
112	./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
113	```
114	
115	## Container-First Rule
116	
117	**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.
118	
119	- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
120	- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
121	- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
122	- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker
123	
124	```bash
125	# Run rspec inside container
126	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec
127	
128	# Run rubocop inside container
129	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop
130	
131	# Rails tasks inside container
132	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
133	```
134	
135	### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)
136	
137	After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.
138	
139	```bash
140	# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
141	docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock
142	
143	# Then rebuild
144	docker compose build auth-api
145	```
146	
147	## API Testing
148	
149	Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.
150

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

**ThatCookiesMine** — a healthcare/medical appointment platform built as a monorepo of independent microservices. Each service has its own `.git` directory and is versioned independently.

## Services & Ports

| Service | Stack | Port | Status |
|---------|-------|------|--------|
| `auth-api/` | Ruby 3.3.6 + Rails 8.0 (API-only) | 3000 | Active |
| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `frontend/` | React 19 + Node 22 LTS | 5173 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `mobile/` | TBD | — | Scaffolded |

Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.

## Infrastructure / Docker

```bash
cd infrastructure
./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
docker-compose logs -f [service]
```

Dev access points when running:
- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
- Admin: http://localhost:3001
- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
- MailHog: http://localhost:8025

## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)

```bash
cd auth-api
bundle install
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
```

### Auth API Architecture

- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`

## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)

```bash
cd admin
npm install
npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
npm run lint      # ESLint
npm run preview   # Preview production build
```

### Admin Architecture

- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod

## Notification Service (Go)

Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).

```bash
cd notification-service
go build ./...
go vet ./...
# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
```

### Notification Service Architecture

- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP

## Dev Setup

Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:

```bash
cd dev-setup
./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
```

## Container-First Rule

**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.

- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker

```bash
# Run rspec inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec

# Run rubocop inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop

# Rails tasks inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
```

### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)

After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.

```bash
# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock

# Then rebuild
docker compose build auth-api
```

## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.

input.old_string:
## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.
input.new_string:
## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.

## New Endpoint Rule

After adding any new endpoint to auth-api, always:

1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
2. Regenerate OpenAPI docs:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
```

Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.

## New Endpoint Rule

After adding any new endpoint to auth-api, always:

1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
2. Regenerate OpenAPI docs:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
```

Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

**ThatCookiesMine** — a healthcare/medical appointment platform built as a monorepo of independent microservices. Each service has its own `.git` directory and is versioned independently.

## Services & Ports

| Service | Stack | Port | Status |
|---------|-------|------|--------|
| `auth-api/` | Ruby 3.3.6 + Rails 8.0 (API-only) | 3000 | Active |
| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `frontend/` | React 19 + Node 22 LTS | 5173 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |
| `mobile/` | TBD | — | Scaffolded |

Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.

## Infrastructure / Docker

```bash
cd infrastructure
./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
docker-compose logs -f [service]
```

Dev access points when running:
- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
- Admin: http://localhost:3001
- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
- MailHog: http://localhost:8025

## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)

```bash
cd auth-api
bundle install
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
```

### Auth API Architecture

- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`

## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)

```bash
cd admin
npm install
npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
npm run lint      # ESLint
npm run preview   # Preview production build
```

### Admin Architecture

- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod

## Notification Service (Go)

Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).

```bash
cd notification-service
go build ./...
go vet ./...
# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
```

### Notification Service Architecture

- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP

## Dev Setup

Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:

```bash
cd dev-setup
./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
```

## Container-First Rule

**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.

- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker

```bash
# Run rspec inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec

# Run rubocop inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop

# Rails tasks inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
```

### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)

After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.

```bash
# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock

# Then rebuild
docker compose build auth-api
```

## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.

+1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
+docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
+Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.
